title: Leads
lede: Leads are your potential customers — every person your AI agents talk to, every form submission, every prospect you import lands here. This guide walks you A-to-Z through the Leads screen: adding, filtering, tagging, tracking lifecycle stages, and converting leads into customers.

steps.title: Open the Leads list
steps.content:
The Leads list is your command center for prospects — and it fills itself: AI agents, booking forms, and imports all create leads here automatically.

  1. In the top navigation, click CRM, then click the Leads tab (or go directly to /crm/leads).
  2. You'll see every lead in a searchable table with columns for Avatar, Name, Email, Phone, Stage, Account, and Last Updated.
  3. Each row also has three action icons on the right: View (eye) opens the lead, Call (phone) dials the lead's first number with the built-in web dialer, and Delete (trash) removes the lead after a confirmation.
  4. Click any lead's name or avatar to open the full Lead Details drawer.

Think of a lead as a person who might buy from you. Once they become a real customer, you convert them into a Contact.

images.alt: Leads list showing the table of leads with stages and phone numbers
images.caption: The Leads list — search, filters, stage pills, and per-row View / Call / Delete actions

images.alt: Leads list showing the table of leads with stages and phone numbers
images.caption: The Leads list — search, filters, stage pills, and per-row View / Call / Delete actions

steps.title: Add a new lead
steps.content:
1. Click Add Lead in the top-right of the list. The Create Lead drawer opens.
  2. Enter the First Name and Last Name (both required).
  3. Click + Add Email to add one or more email addresses — the first one becomes the primary email.
  4. Click + Add Phone Number to add a phone with a label (Mobile, Home, Work, Office, and more). You can add several numbers.
  5. Pick a Source (where this lead came from) and a Stage — new leads default to New.
  6. Optionally link an Account (the lead's company) and fill in the address fields: Street Address, Apartment/Suite, City, State/Province, Postal Code, Country.
  7. Click Save.
💡 You rarely have to add leads one by one — the Import button (upload icon next to Add Lead) bulk-imports a CSV, and your AI agents create leads automatically during calls.

images.alt: Create Lead drawer with name, emails, phone numbers, source, stage, account and address fields
images.caption: The Create Lead drawer — only First and Last Name are required

images.alt: Create Lead drawer with name, emails, phone numbers, source, stage, account and address fields
images.caption: The Create Lead drawer — only First and Last Name are required

steps.title: Search, filter, and sort
steps.content:
Find any lead in seconds, even with thousands in the list:

  • Search box — use the dropdown on its left to switch the search field between Name, Email, and Phone. Phone search is forgiving: it matches digits even if the stored number is formatted differently.
  • Filter By — a multi-select where you can stack filters: Stage, Account, Source, Tags, Address, City, and Owner. Picking a filter reveals a dedicated input next to it (for example a stage picker or a tag dropdown).
  • Extra columns — selecting the Source, Tags, Address, or City filter also adds that column to the table so you can see the value on every row.
  • Sorting — click a column header (Name, Email, Stage, Account, Last Updated) to sort; click again to flip the direction.
  • API Info — the link icon at the far right of the toolbar opens a window with ready-made API examples for creating and reading leads programmatically. Pair it with your key from API Keys.

images.alt: Leads toolbar with search field selector, Search Leads box, Filter By dropdown and action buttons
images.caption: The toolbar: search field picker, Filter By, Select all, Import, Add Lead, Find Duplicates, and API Info

images.alt: Leads toolbar with search field selector, Search Leads box, Filter By dropdown and action buttons
images.caption: The toolbar: search field picker, Filter By, Select all, Import, Add Lead, Find Duplicates, and API Info

steps.title: Open a lead — the Lead Details drawer
steps.content:
Click any lead to open its full profile in a drawer. Everything about the person lives here:

  • Header — the avatar (click the camera to upload a photo), the lead's name, the current stage badge, and quick-action circles to Call Lead and Send Email.
  • Lead Information tab — edit Emails (multiple, first is primary), Account, Tags (type a tag and press Enter), Phone Numbers (add with a label, delete with the trash icon), and Timezone.
  • Convert To Contact — the button in the drawer's top-right promotes this lead to your Contacts list once they become a customer.
💡 Set the lead's Timezone — campaign calling windows and calendar events respect it, so you never ring someone at 3 AM.

images.alt: Lead Details drawer showing header, tabs and Lead Information fields
images.caption: The Lead Details drawer — header with quick actions, tabs, and the Lead Information fields

images.alt: Lead Details drawer showing header, tabs and Lead Information fields
images.caption: The Lead Details drawer — header with quick actions, tabs, and the Lead Information fields

steps.title: Explore the rest of the tabs
steps.content:
The drawer's tab bar covers the lead's whole world. Besides Lead Information you'll find:

  • Additional Information — first/last name, the full address (Street Address, Apartment/Suite, City, State/Province, Postal Code, Country), plus a Tax-Exempt toggle with an Exemption Certificate # field for tax-free customers.
  • Files — attach documents, photos, or contracts to the lead.
  • Calendar — every calendar event linked to this lead, with an Add Event button to book the next appointment.
  • Email — a full email thread view: send emails to the lead and read replies without leaving the CRM. Badges on the tab show total and unread counts.
  • Deals, Invoices, Quotes, Jobs — everything in your pipeline tied to this lead.
  • Payment Methods — cards saved on file for this lead.

images.alt: Additional Information tab with name, address fields and Tax-Exempt toggle
images.caption: Additional Information — address details and the Tax-Exempt toggle

images.alt: Additional Information tab with name, address fields and Tax-Exempt toggle
images.caption: Additional Information — address details and the Tax-Exempt toggle

steps.title: Review every call in Call Logs
steps.content:
This is where the AI magic becomes visible: every call with this lead — AI agent calls, campaign calls, and calls your team makes with the web dialer — is logged automatically.

  1. Open the Call Logs tab in the Lead Details drawer.
  2. Each row shows the Date, the Type (an AI Dialer pill for AI-agent calls, a Web Dialer pill for calls your team placed), the Previous Call Transition (how that call moved the lead's lifecycle stage — for example New ▶ Interested), the Number dialed, and the call's Duration.
  3. Click any row to open the full call record — recording, transcript, and the AI's post-call summary.
💡 No more "what did we talk about?" — the complete conversation history for every lead is one click away.

images.alt: Call Logs tab listing calls with date, type, number and duration columns
images.caption: Call Logs — every AI and human call with this lead, clickable for full details

images.alt: Call Logs tab listing calls with date, type, number and duration columns
images.caption: Call Logs — every AI and human call with this lead, clickable for full details

steps.title: Store Custom Data your AI agents can use
steps.content:
The Custom Data tab holds custom variables on the lead — extra fields like gate code, preferred technician, or membership level that don't have a built-in field.

  1. Open the Custom Data tab.
  2. Click + Add Field, type a Field Name and a Value, then click Save.
  3. Edit or delete fields any time from the Actions column.

These variables are shared with your AI agents: an agent can read them to personalize the conversation ("I see your gate code is 4321, I'll pass it to the technician") and can write new ones it learns during a call. Learn more in Custom Variables.

images.alt: Custom Data tab with Add Field button and field name/value inputs
images.caption: Custom Data — free-form fields your AI agents can read and update

images.alt: Custom Data tab with Add Field button and field name/value inputs
images.caption: Custom Data — free-form fields your AI agents can read and update

steps.title: Track the lifecycle and keep notes
steps.content:
Scroll below the tabs and you'll find two more sections:

  • Lifecycle — a stage bar showing your pipeline (for example New → Contacted → Interested → Qualified → Negotiation → Won). Click a stage to move the lead there; stages with a small arrow have sub-stages to choose from. The current stage is highlighted and also colors the badge in the header. You customize these stages in CRM Settings.
  • Campaign Lifecycles — if the lead is part of a calling campaign, a separate per-campaign stage bar appears here, updated automatically as the AI works the lead.
  • Notes — type in Add your note and press Enter. Notes are timestamped and stack up as a running journal on the lead.
⚠️ Moving a lead to a stage like Won or Lost doesn't convert or delete it — it just records where the lead stands. Use Convert To Contact when they actually become a customer.

images.alt: Lifecycle stage bar with New, Contacted, Interested, Qualified, Negotiation and Won stages
images.caption: The Lifecycle bar — click a stage to move the lead through your pipeline

images.alt: Lifecycle stage bar with New, Contacted, Interested, Qualified, Negotiation and Won stages
images.caption: The Lifecycle bar — click a stage to move the lead through your pipeline

images.alt: Notes section with the Add your note input
images.caption: Notes — a timestamped journal on every lead

images.alt: Notes section with the Add your note input
images.caption: Notes — a timestamped journal on every lead

steps.title: Bulk actions — convert, reassign, or delete many at once
steps.content:
1. Tick the checkbox on each lead's row, or tick Select all N leads in the toolbar to select every lead matching your current search and filters (across all pages).
  2. A Selected N counter appears with the bulk buttons:
  • Convert To Contact — promotes the selected leads into your Contacts list (available when rows are hand-picked).
  • Change Owner (person icon) — reassigns the selected leads to another team member.
  • Assign Account (card icon) — links the selected leads to a company account, or clears the link.
  • Delete Selected (red trash icon) — removes them all after a confirmation.
💡 Combine filters with Select all for power moves: filter by Tag = trade-show-2026, select all, and assign every one of them to a teammate in two clicks.

images.alt: Toolbar with Select all leads checkbox, Selected counter and bulk action buttons
images.caption: Bulk mode — Convert To Contact, Change Owner, Assign Account, and Delete Selected

images.alt: Toolbar with Select all leads checkbox, Selected counter and bulk action buttons
images.caption: Bulk mode — Convert To Contact, Change Owner, Assign Account, and Delete Selected

steps.title: Find and remove duplicate leads
steps.content:
Imports and inbound calls can create the same person twice. Clean up in seconds:

  1. Click the Find Duplicates button (copy icon) in the toolbar. The Find & Remove Duplicate Leads window opens.
  2. Choose the Duplicate scope: across all leads, or only within the same account.
  3. Click Phone Number or Email Address to scan for leads sharing that value.
  4. Matching groups appear below — delete duplicates per group, or delete all at once. The oldest lead in each group is always kept, so your original record and its history survive.
⚠️ Deleting duplicates cannot be undone. Skim the groups before confirming a bulk delete.

images.alt: Find and Remove Duplicate Leads window with scope selector and Phone Number / Email Address buttons
images.caption: Find & Remove Duplicate Leads — scan by phone or email, keep the oldest record

images.alt: Find and Remove Duplicate Leads window with scope selector and Phone Number / Email Address buttons
images.caption: Find & Remove Duplicate Leads — scan by phone or email, keep the oldest record

steps.title: How your AI agents work with leads
steps.content:
Leads aren't just a list you maintain by hand — they're the memory of your AI call center:

  • AI agents create leads — when an unknown caller reaches your agent (or your agent is told to capture someone's details), it saves them as a new lead with name, phone, and whatever it learned.
  • AI agents update leads — during a call the agent can update fields and Custom Data variables, and every call is written to the lead's Call Logs automatically.
  • Campaigns work your leads — add leads to a dial list and a campaign calls through them, advancing each lead's Campaign Lifecycle as it goes. See Create a Campaign.
  • Personalization — agent prompts can reference lead fields and custom variables (like the lead's first name), so every conversation feels personal. See Custom Variables.

Set your stages once, let the AI fill and work the list, and watch your pipeline move on its own.

support.content:
